summary: Collapsed Gin route wiring into a single router file and enabled explicit JSON 405 responses for method mismatches.
reason: The top-level Gin setup was split between router and routes packages without adding much value. Consolidating the wiring makes route registration easier to audit and improves API behavior by distinguishing unknown routes from wrong HTTP methods.
risk: LOW — route definitions were moved, not redesigned. Behavior change is limited to returning 405 JSON for method mismatches instead of the previous ambiguous 404.
notes: GET /api/v1/auth/login from a browser is not a valid call because the endpoint is POST-only. After this change, that request returns 405 Method Not Allowed instead of 404 Not Found.
